name: What's the time limit to claim comp for slow-developing lung damage from my job?

mainEntity.name: What's the time limit to claim comp for slow-developing lung damage from my job?

acceptedAnswer.text: Usually 1-2 years from diagnosis or disability awareness. Don't delay[5].
acceptedAnswer.description: File within 1-2 years from when you knew or should have known the illness was work-related, such as diagnosis date—deadlines are strict in most states including Utah. Occupational respiratory diseases like COPD from cumulative exposure have 'discovery' rules, but delays risk denial[5]. Ohio example: 1 year from disability or 6 months from diagnosis. - Track symptoms and doctor visits from onset. - Cumulative trauma claims start clock at substantial disability. Act fast upon diagnosis, document timeline, and consult an attorney to avoid missing cutoffs.

description: Usually 1-2 years from diagnosis or disability awareness. Don't delay[5].
